# Claude Code Security Best Practices. Claude Code is a popular agentic coding assistant designed by Anthropic to run in a developer’s terminal, offering deep codebase analysis, code generation, project planning, and automation through natural language prompts. ## **Why Claude Code Security Matters**. When AI becomes part of your coding environment, the IDE is no longer “just a text editor.” It’s an insider with:. * **The ability to run commands**. That’s not just convenience — it’s risk. A single poisoned prompt or misconfigured setting can turn Claude Code from your coding partner into a threat actor. This blog is your guide to staying safe: how to configure Claude Code, what traps to avoid, and how to combine internal and external defenses. Every time it runs a command or reads a file, it’s acting with your permissions. ## **The Heart of Security: `managed-settings.json`**. Like most AI coders, Claude Code has a variety of configuration settings for security. Claude Code’s security settings live here:.

title: 💻 Mastering the Vibe: Claude Code Best Practices That Actually Work | by Dinanjana Gunaratne | Medium
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

*How I went from fumbling with AI-generated spaghetti code to building production-ready apps with Claude Code*. Three months ago, I was that developer who would throw a vague prompt at Claude Code, cross my fingers, and hope for the best. If you’re ready to move beyond the “ask and pray” approach to something that actually works consistently, here’s what I’ve learned about making Claude Code genuinely useful. Think of it as Claude’s memory bank for your project. This isn’t just configuration — it’s how you teach Claude to understand your specific codebase, conventions, and quirks. The magic isn’t in the format — there’s no required format for CLAUDE.md files — it’s in being specific about what matters for *your* project. But the real power comes from asking Claude to make a plan before coding and explicitly telling it not to code until you’ve confirmed that its plan looks good.

title: 💻 Claude Code power user tips
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

This article collects workflow tips from the Claude Code team at Anthropic. These practices cover parallel execution, planning, automation, verification, and customization—the patterns the team uses every day to ship code faster. Everyone’s setup is different, so experiment to see what works for you. **Important:** The single most impactful tip in this guide is **verification**—giving Claude a way to check its own output. These are power-user patterns collected from individual engineers on the Claude Code team. * Most commands shown here ship with Claude Code: /color and /btw are **built-in commands**, and /simplify and /loop are **bundled skills** that ship with the CLI. See the **commands reference** and **skills**. You can build your own skills by adding a SKILL.md file under .claude/skills//. Community plugins (for example the “ralph-wiggum” plugin) are not reviewed or sanctioned by Anthropic — check with your administrator before installing third-party plugins in a managed environment. Everything else in this guide works on a stock Claude Code install.

# Batch Processing. Optimizes large-scale task execution through agent swarms, API batch processing, and intelligent loops to maximize efficiency and reduce costs. ### Related Skills. Use when the user wants to create one or many images from data (CSV/list), reuse a visual template with different texts/photos, or batch-produce cards for a marketplace, social network, or event. Manages Discord operations including messaging, reactions, and channel management directly through Claude. Browse the MCP Market to get started. The Batch Processing skill for Claude Code empowers developers to handle high-volume tasks—such as reviewing dozens of files or processing hundreds of documents—without linear increases in time or token costs. It provides a strategic framework for selecting the optimal execution method based on scale: utilizing agent swarms for rapid parallel processing of 5-20 items, the Anthropic API Batch for 50% cost savings on massive datasets, or sequential loops for tasks with complex dependencies. Sync skills to **Claude Cowork**, **Claude Code**, **Codex**, and more.

title: 💻 What Is Claude Code /simplify and /batch? The Built-In Commands That Fix Overengineering | MindStudio
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

# What Is Claude Code /simplify and /batch? Claude Code's /simplify and /batch commands help you clean up bloated code and run large refactors in parallel. Claude Code tackles this with two commands worth knowing: `/simplify` and `/batch`. The `/batch` command lets you run operations across multiple files simultaneously, so large refactors don’t require running the same prompt over and over. Here’s how both work and when to use them. ## What Claude Code Is. Claude Code is Anthropic’s terminal-based AI coding assistant. It connects to your codebase through the file system, which means it can see the actual state of your project rather than relying on what you paste into a chat window. Instead of writing a long prompt explaining what you want, you type a command and Claude handles the task with the right behavior built in. When you run `/simplify` on a file or function, Claude Code evaluates:.

| |  | | --- | | Show HN: Send Claude Code tasks to the Batch API at 50% off (github.com/s2-streamstore) | | 23 points by misker1 4 months ago | hide | past | favorite | 1 comment | | Hey HN. I built this because my Anthropic API bills were getting out of hand (spoiler: they remain high even with this, batch is not a magic bullet). So here is a small MCP that lets you send work directly to Anthropic's Batch API from inside Claude Code, for the same quality responses just 50% cheaper, results come back in ~30min-1hr. ```  How it works: you type /batch review this codebase for security issues, Claude gathers all the context, builds a self-contained prompt, ships it to the Batch API via an MCP server, and you get notified in the status bar when it's done (optional). I removed the curl | bash setup and at this stage of the project i feel more confident sharing the manual setup instructions.

title: 💻 Claude Code /simplify and /batch Commands Guide
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

# Claude Code /simplify and /batch: Bundled Commands That Ship Code Faster. Claude Code /simplify runs 3-agent code review on your changes. The code works, but you know there's duplicated logic, a few functions that could be cleaner, and some inefficient patterns you didn't have time to address. # After implementing a feature # After implementing a feature/simplify/simplify  # Focus on a specific concern # Focus on a specific concern/simplify focus on memory efficiency/simplify  focus  on  memory  efficiency. If you need something bigger -- migrating an entire codebase from one framework to another, or applying a change across dozens of files -- that's what `/batch` is for. ## What Claude Code /simplify Does. Boris Cherny, the Claude Code PM, put it well: these bundled commands "automate much of the work it used to take to shepherd a pull request to production." `/simplify` is the automated code review step between "it works" and "it's ready to merge.".
